title: Interior vs Architecture: What Comes First and Why It Changes Everything
author: CITRA Associates
metaDescription: Interior design and architecture must align from the start. Learn what each discipline does, which comes first, and how coordinating them saves money and avoids costly reworks.
excerpt: Architecture and interior design are most effective when coordinated from the brief stage. This article explains what each discipline covers, why sequence matters, the financial cost of getting the order wrong, and why integrated practices produce the most coherent results.

faqs.question: Should I hire an architect or interior designer first?
faqs.answer: The architect should always come first for new construction or major renovation projects. The architect determines the building's structure, layout, openings, and systems — all of which create the canvas on which the interior designer works. Starting with interior design before the architecture is fixed leads to conflicts that require expensive changes to either the building fabric or the interior scheme.

faqs.question: Can one person be both an architect and interior designer in India?
faqs.answer: Yes. Many architects in India practice interior design as part of their service offering, and some firms provide fully integrated architecture and interior design services under one roof. This integration is beneficial because it eliminates the coordination gaps that arise when two separate teams work on the same project without consistent communication.

faqs.question: What is the difference between an interior designer and an interior decorator in India?
faqs.answer: An interior designer has formal training in space planning, materials, lighting, ergonomics, and building systems. They can coordinate with architects and contractors on substantive changes to a space. An interior decorator focuses on the aesthetic treatment of existing spaces — furniture, colour, soft furnishings — without necessarily having the technical background to manage structural or systems changes.
